dict.string: Close Settings, then open it again and tap Jot. Or open the Jot app, go to Settings → Privacy → Full Access, and follow the breadcrumb: General → Keyboard → Keyboards → Jot. Turn on Allow Full Access there, then come back.

Nothing you say or type ever leaves your iPhone.*
Full keyboard access lets Jot use your microphone to turn your speech into text in real time — that's all it's used for. It never reads or sends what you type.
You can confirm all of this yourself in Apple's App Privacy Report (Settings → Privacy & Security → App Privacy Report).

*The one exception is the Send Feedback form in the Jot app's Settings: if you deliberately open it and send feedback, only that feedback leaves your device — nothing else, ever. Nothing is sent automatically.
